Study Hub / Pak Study / Testpoint / Lesson 8

Lesson 8

Questions 1401–1600 · Tap a card to reveal the explanation.

1

Who was the only Vice President in the history of Pakistan?

Correct Answer: Nur-ul-Amin
Show explanation
Explanation

  • Full Name: Nurul Amin

  • Born: 15 July 1893, Shahbazpur, Bengal Presidency, British India

  • Died: 2 October 1974 (aged 81), Rawalpindi, Punjab, Pakistan

  • Eighth Prime Minister of Pakistan:

    • Term: 7 December 1971 – 20 December 1971

    • Duration: 13 days (shortest tenure of any Pakistani Prime Minister)

  • First and Only Vice President of Pakistan:

    • Term: 20 December 1971 – 14 August 1973

    Last Updated on 12-01-2026

    2

    Who was the first muslim governor of the province Punjab was?

    Correct Answer: Abdur Rab Nishtar
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • First Muslim Governor of Punjab: August 1949 – November 1951
    • Appointed Governor of Punjab after the dismissal of the Punjab provincial government in 1949.

    Additional information:
    • Born: 13 June 1899, Peshawar
    • Died: 14 February 1958 (aged 58), Karachi

    Last Updated on 12-01-2026

    3

    The Chairman of National Economic Council is _____?

    Correct Answer: Prime Minister
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    • The National Economic Council (NEC) of Pakistan is chaired by the Prime Minister.

    • It is a constitutional body formed under Article 156 of the Constitution of Pakistan.

    • The council is responsible for economic planning and development policies.

    NEC 5 Key Members:

    • Prime Minister – Chairman

    • Chief Ministers of Punjab, Sindh, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Balochistan

    • Federal Ministers related to finance and planning


      Last Updated on 12-01-2026

    4

    River Kabul joins Indus at?

    Correct Answer: Attock
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • River Kabul flows from Afghanistan into Khyber Pakhtunkhwa before joining Indus.
    • River Kabul passes through Kabul (Afghanistan), Jalalabad, and Peshawar.

      Additional information:
    • Indus River – Longest river of Pakistan
    • Jhelum River – Joins Indus at Trimmu
    • Chenab River – Joins Indus at Panjnad
    • Ravi River – Tributary of Indus
    • Sutlej River – Joins Indus at Panjnad

    Last Updated on 12-01-2026

    5

    The Mausoleum of Khawja Ghulam Farid is located in ____?

    Correct Answer: Mithankot (Rajanpur)
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Born: 1845, Chachran, Bahawalpur, Punjab, Pakistan
      • Died: 24 July 1901 (aged 56), Chachran, Bahawalpur
      • Resting Place / Mausoleum: Mithankot, Punjab
      • Sufi Order: Member of Chishti Nizami Sufi order

      Last Updated on 12-01-2026
    6

    When karakorum Highway was completed?

    Correct Answer: 1979
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The Karakoram Highway (KKH), also called the "Eighth Wonder of the World".
    • It was completed in 1979.
    • Construction started in 1966 and took 13 years to complete.
    • It connects Pakistan (Gilgit-Baltistan) with China (Xinjiang) through the Karakoram mountain range.
    • The highway is 1,300 km long.

    Last Updated on 12-01-2026

    7

    Shandur Pass connects which of the following?

    Correct Answer: Chitral with Gilgit
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    Shandur Pass is a high mountain pass at an altitude of 3,700 meters (12,140 ft).
    It connects the Chitral District of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa with Gilgit-Baltistan.
    Famous for the annual Shandur Polo Festival, held every July.

    Additional Points:
    Shandur is known as the “Roof of the World” because of its high altitude.
    The pass remains snowbound in winter.
    It is usually open from June to September.

    Last Updated on 12-01-2026
    8

    Faiz Ahmed Faiz was arrested in Rawalpindi Conspiracy Case in ______?

    Correct Answer: 1951
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Faiz Ahmed Faiz, a renowned Pakistani poet and intellectual.

    He was arrested in 1951 in connection with the Rawalpindi Conspiracy Case.

    This political conspiracy was alleged by the government of Liaquat Ali Khan.

    The case involved allegations of a coup plot against the government.

    9

    Ahmad Khan Kharal the famous character of War of Independence belongs to _____?

    Correct Answer: Jhamra
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Ahmad Khan Kharal was a leading freedom fighter of the War of Independence (1857).

    He led resistance against British rule in Punjab.

    He belonged to Jhamra.

    Jhamra is a village of the Faisalabad District in Punjab, Pakistan.


    Additional Details / Facts

    He played a key role in uprisings around Gogera region.

    Martyred while fighting the British in 1857.

    10

    Mustafa Kamal abolished Khilafat in _____?

    Correct Answer: 1924
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Mustafa Kemal Atatürk abolished the Khilafat (Caliphate) in 1924.

    This was part of his modernization and secularization reforms in Turkey.

    The abolition marked the end of the Ottoman Caliphate system.


    Additional Details / Facts

    Capital of Turkey moved from Istanbul to Ankara.

    Introduced Western-style legal and educational systems.

    Paved the way for the Republic of Turkey.

    11

    Rakh Ghulaman Life Stock Farm is located in _____?

    Correct Answer: Bhakkar
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Rakh Ghulaman Livestock Farm is located in Bhakkar, Pakistan.

    It is a large livestock breeding and research farm.

    Focuses on cattle, sheep, and goat farming.


    Additional Details / Facts

    Supports dairy and meat production in Pakistan.

    Contributes to agricultural research and livestock improvement.

    Managed under Punjab Livestock & Dairy Development Department.

    12

    ‘Naxalite’ movement is a pro communism movement started in?

    Correct Answer: India
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The ‘Naxalite’ movement is a pro-communist armed rebellion

    Began in 1967 in the Naxalbari village of West Bengal, India.

    History 

    - In 1967, a faction of the Communist Party of India (Marxist) broke away, known as the "Siliguri Group".  

    - The group was led by Charu Majumdar, Kanu Sanyal, and Jangal Santhal.  

    - They launched a movement for a protracted people's war in India, inspired by the Chinese Communist Revolution.  

    - Charu Majumdar wrote the Historic Eight Documents, which became the ideological foundation of the Naxalite Movement.  

    - The movement began in Naxalbari, West Bengal — hence the name “Naxalite”.

    13

    Who was the founder of the All-India Muslim League?

    Correct Answer: Nawab Khwaja Salimullah
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • The All India Muslim League was founded on 30 December 1906 in Dacca, British India (now Dhaka, Bangladesh).
      • It's founder was Nawab Khwaja Salimullah.
      • It's first President was Sir Agha Khan.
      • The headquarters of the All India Muslim League was in Lucknow.
      • The party was dissolved on 14 August 1947.

      When the Muslim League ended in 1947, it split into three parties:

      1. Muslim League in Pakistan
      2. Awami League in Bangladesh
      3. Indian Union Muslim League in India
      14

      Which of the following provinces of Afghanistan share border with Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Ningrahar, Paktia, Ghazni
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The following provinces of Afghanistan share a border with Pakistan:-

      Eastern Border (Khyber Pakhtunkhwa region of Pakistan):

      1. Nangarhar: Borders Pakistan's Khyber Pakhtunkhwa; a major crossing point is Torkham. 
      2. Kunar: Also borders Pakistan's KP. 
      3. Paktia & Khost: Share borders with Pakistan's KP. 
      4. Paktika: Borders Pakistan's Balochistan and KP. 

      Southern Border (Balochistan region of Pakistan):

      1. Zabul: Borders Pakistan's Balochistan. 
      2. Kandahar: Borders Pakistan's Balochistan, with the Spin Boldak crossing. 
      3. Helmand: Borders Pakistan's Balochistan. 
      4. Nimruz: Also borders Pakistan's Balochistan. 
      15

      Working boundary is the border between Pakistan and _______?

      Correct Answer: Indian-occupied Kashmir
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      It is the line between Pakistan along Sialkot and Indian-held Kashmir.

      It is called a working boundary because:

      • on one side is internationally recognized land (Sialkot)
      • on the other is a disputed territory. 



      16

      Which is the largest/biggest barrage of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Sukkur
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Sukkur Barrage is the largest in Pakistan.

      It Is the pride of Pakistan's Irrigation system and in the whole world.


      Additional information:

      Sukkur Barrage is a barrage on the River Indus 

      The barrage was built during the British Raj

      Build from 1923 to 1932 

      Its old name was named Lloyd Barrage.

      It is located in Sindh Province

      17

      Before the partition of India in 1947 how many Princely states existed?

      Correct Answer: 562
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      The largest Princely state by area at the time of partition was Kashmir.

      Out of 562 princely states during 1947, 13 princely states decided to join Pakistan.

      ****

      These 13 princely states are:

          Bahawalpur: On 3 October 1947, the Nawab of Bahawalpur, Sadeq Mohammad Khan V, acceded his state to Pakistan, becoming the first ruler to do so successfully.

          Khairpur: The state of Khairpur acceded to Pakistan on 3 October 1947.

          Chitral: The Mehtar of Chitral, Muzaffar-ul-Mulk, stated his intention to accede to Pakistan on 15 August 1947. On 28 July 1969, President Yahya Khan announced the full integration

            RK15.06.2023

            18

            The height of K-2 the second highest mountain is _______ feet.

            Correct Answer: 28,251 feet
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            ·         K2 also called Mount Godwin Austen, called locally Dapsang or Chogori,

            ·         The world's second-highest peak

            ·         K2 height in feet 28,251

            ·         K2 height in meters 8,611 meters

            ·         K2 second only to Mount Everest.

            19

            The members of constituent assembly was increased from 69 to _____?

            Correct Answer: 79
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            The membership of the Constituent Assembly was increased from 69 to 79 in 1948.
            This allowed additional representatives from various regions of Pakistan.
            The assembly worked under the Government of India Act 1935 initially.
            It played a key role in framing Pakistan’s first constitution in 1956.
            20

            Who wrote the book ‘Hujjat Allah Al Baligha’?

            Correct Answer: Shah Wali Ullah
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Conclusive Argument From God (Hujjat Allah al Baligha)

            The Conclusive Argument of God is the masterwork of Shah Wallah of Delhi (1762)

            Originally published on: 1979

            Author of Hujjat Allah al Baligha: Shah Waliullah Dehlawi

            Editor of Hujjat Allah al Baligha : Marcia Hermansen

            ****

            Who is the Current Governor of Punjab?

            Correct Answer: Sardar Saleem Haider
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Sardar Saleem Haider is the current and 37th Governor Punjab

            Appointment date: 10 May 2024

            Party name: PPP

            Predecessor: Baligh ur Rehman 

            Last Verified and Updated on 11-03-2026

            22

            Where is Katas Raj temple (Qila Katas) located?

            Correct Answer: Chakwal
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • The Shri Katas Raj Temples is also known as Qila Katas.
            • The temple is located in Choa Saidanshah, Chakwal, near the M2 Motorway.
            • The temples were established by the Hindu Shahis between the mid-7th and 10th centuries.

            23

            "Aab e Hayat " was written by?

            Correct Answer: Muhammad Hussain Azad
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Muhammad Husain Azad was a scholar and an Urdu writer.

            who wrote both prose and poetry.

            His best known work is Aab-e-Hayat. 

            Born: May 5, 1830, Delhi, India

            Died: January 22, 1910, Lahore

            24

            The length of Siachen glacier is _____?

            Correct Answer: 47 miles
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Siachen Glacier is approximately 76 km long, which is equal to 47 miles.

            It is located in the Karakoram Range of the Himalayas.

            It is the highest battlefield in the world.

            The glacier is strategically important between India and Pakistan.

            The glacier lies at an altitude of 17,000 to 22,000 feet.

            Siachen is called the “Third Pole” due to its extensive ice coverage.

            25

            Where is lake Kachura situated in Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Skardu
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Kachura Lakes are three lakes in the Skardu, Gilgit Baltistan.

            The lakes, at 2,500 metres in elevation.

            They are Upper Kachura Lake and Lower Kachura Lake.

            26

            Jallianwala Bagh is located in _____?

            Correct Answer: Ameristar
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Jallianwala Bagh Massacre took place on 13 April 1919

            In Jallianwala Bagh massacre killing at least 379 people and injuring over 1,200 other people.

            Jallianwala Bagh is a historic garden close to the Golden Temple complex in Amritsar, Punjab, India

            By General Reginald Dyer order did the massacre take place in Jallianwala Bagh

             It is managed by the Jallianwala Bagh National Memorial Trust and was renovated between 2019 and 2021****

            nd9-2-2023


            27

            Jallianwala Bagh Massacre took place on?

            Correct Answer: 13 April 1919
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Jallianwala Bagh massacre, also known as the Amritsar massacre

            Jallianwala Bagh Massacre took place on 13 April 1919

            In Jallianwala Bagh massacre killing at least 379 people and injured over 1,200 other people.

            Jallianwala Bagh is in Amritsar  Punjab, India

            By General Reginald Dyer's order did the massacre take place in Jallianwala Bagh

            NOW

            Jallianwala Bagh is managed by the Jallianwala Bagh National Memorial Trust and was renovated between 2019 and 2021


            ****

            ND16-3-2023

            28

            Which cities of Iran and Pakistan are linked through railway line?

            Correct Answer: Quetta-Zedan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pakistan and Iran are linked by the railway line connecting Quetta (Pakistan) to Zahedan (Iran)

            Pakistan: The line runs from Quetta through Taftan to the Iranian border. 

            Iran: The line continues from Mirjaveh (border) to Zahedan, connecting to the Iranian network. 

            29

            Doctrine of necessity was used in the case for the first time in the history of Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din Vs State case
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Doctrine of Necessity was first used in Maulvi Tamiz-ud-Din vs State (1955).

            It was applied to validate the action of dissolving the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan.



            30

            Who was Pakistan 1st ambassador to the United Nations Organizations ?

            Correct Answer: Patris bukhari
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Dr. Zafarullah Khan was Pakistan’s first ambassador to the United Nations in 1947.

            He played a key role in representing Pakistan at the UN after independence.

            He later served as the President of the UN General Assembly in 1962.

            He was also Pakistan’s Foreign Minister from 1954 to 1955.

            His efforts helped establish Pakistan’s early diplomatic presence internationally.

            **

            Pakistan Ambassador List 

            On July 31, 2020, Mr. Moin ul Haque is the current ambassador of Pakistan to China 

            Mansoor Khan has been the Current Ambassador of Pakistan to Afghanistan since 2020.

            Lt. Gen. Bilal Akbar is the new ambassador of Pakistan to Saudi Arabia

            Ambassador Zahoor Ahmedis the current Ambassador of Pakistan to the UK 2022

            Mr. Ahmed Amjad Ali, Ambassador of Pakistan in Iraq

            Ambassador Akram is the current ambassador of Pakistan to the UN

            The current Pakistani Ambassador to Afghanistan is Obaid ur Rehman Nizamani.

            ***



            31

            Al-Hilal weekly was started by____________?

            Correct Answer: Abul Kalam Azad
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Al-Hilal in 1912 was Founder Maulana Abul Kalam Azad.

            Launched: July 13, 1912.
            Language: Urdu.

            The newspaper was published in Urdu from Calcutta. 

            Significance: It brought a new era to Urdu journalism with its anti-British stance and support for Indian self-rule.
            Closure: Banned by the British government in 1914 under the Press Act.
            32

            Pakistan State Bank started working on 1st July 1948 with the help of Rs?

            Correct Answer: Thirty Millions
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pakistan State Bank started working on 1st July 1948 with the amount of Rs: Thirty Millions.

            History of State Bank

            It was inaugurated on 1st July 1948.

            The headquarters is in Karachi, Sindh

            Zahid Husain was the founder and the first governor of the SBP from June 1948 to July 1953.

            Dr. Shamshad Akhtar was the first female Governor of the SBP in 2006.

            Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah inaugurated the State Bank of Pakistan.

            The Governor is appointed by the President of Pakistan.

            Term of five years.

            33

            What is the main cause of failure of Pakistan India Iran gas pipeline project?

            Correct Answer: Economic and political pressure of USA
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Pakistan-India-Iran gas pipeline project was mainly failed due to economic and political pressure from the USA.

            The project was officially cancelled around 2016.

            34

            Who is the author of the book Struggle For Pakistan"?

            Correct Answer: I.H. Qureshi
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            'I.H. Qureshi is the author of the book "struggle of Pakistan"

            **

            Ayesha Jalal wrote the book titled: The Struggle for Pakistan:

            while I.H. Qureshi wrote the book: The Struggle for Pakistan

             Publish Date. 1965

              35

              Which of the following king's tomb is not situated in sub continent?

              Correct Answer: Babar
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The tomb of Babur is located in the Gardens of Babur in Kabul, Afghanistan - (Not part of Indian Subcontinent)  

              - Jahangir – Tomb in Lahore, Pakistan (Subcontinent)  

              - Akbar – Tomb in Sikandra, Agra, India (Subcontinent)  

              - Aurangzeb – Tomb in Khuldabad, Maharashtra, India (Subcontinent)

              36

              Where Akhori Dam is located?

              Correct Answer: Punjab
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Akhori Dam is located near Akhori Village in Attock District of Punjab.

              Situated on Nandna Kas, a tributary of the Haro River.

              Purpose: Water storage, hydropower generation, and flood control. 

              It has been proposed by the former Pakistan Muslim League (Q)'s Government.

              Developed by WAPDA as part of its Water Vision 2025, 

              It will also host a 600 MW hydropower plant

              Length 5.16 km

              37

              Who is the writer of the book "India wins freedom"?

              Correct Answer: Abul Kalam Azad
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Molana Abul Kalam Azad was the author of  "The book India Wins Freedom". 

              India Wins Freedom was published on 1 January 1988.

              It was the introduction of the Indian Act of 1935 and different events of the subcontinent.

              38

              Who was the 1st CM of the Punjab?

              Correct Answer: Iftikhar Hussain mamdot
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The first Chief Minister of Punjab (West Punjab, Pakistan) after independence was Nawab Iftikhar Hussain Khan Mamdot

              He held his office from 15 Aug 1947 to 25 Jan 1949

              Party: Pakistan Muslim League.

              Born on 31 December 1906, Lahore

              Died on 16 October 1969 (aged 62) in Lahore 

              Cause of death: influenza


              History:

              Leader of Opposition of Punjab: 1946–1947

              6th Governor of Sindh: 24 June 1954 – 14 October 1955

              39

              Babar came to India originally from _____?

              Correct Answer: Furghana
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Babur, the founder of the Mughal Empire, originally came from the Fergana Valley, 

              It is a region in Central Asia that is part of modern-day Uzbekistan.

              He was a Timurid prince

              First Mughal king, 1526-1530.

              Babar ruled India from 1526 to 1530.

              Babar was the first Mughal king to rule India.

              Descended from Timur (father's side) and Genghis Khan (mother's side). 



              40

              Who was Pakistan’s first transgender news anchor?

              Correct Answer: Marvia Malik
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Marvia Malik became Pakistan’s first transgender news anchor when she appeared on air for Kohenoor TV in 2018.

              She made history as a transgender broadcast journalist and media figure in Pakistan.

              41

              Before ISI, which intelligence agency was established in Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Intelligence Bureau (IB)
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Intelligence Bureau (IB) was established in 1947, immediately after Pakistan’s independence.

              It was the primary intelligence agency before the creation of ISI (Inter-Services Intelligence) in 1948.

              42

              When did Pakistan hold its first referendum?

              Correct Answer: 1965
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan held its first referendum in January 1965 to endorse Ayub Khan’s presidency.

              It was conducted under the 1962 Constitution, marking the first nationwide vote in the country.

              43

              The largest oil-producing field in Pakistan is ______?

              Correct Answer: Sui
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Sui Gas Field in Balochistan is Pakistan’s largest natural gas and oil-producing field.

              It has been a major contributor to the country’s energy supply since its discovery in 1952.

              44

              What was the first television channel launched in Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: PTV
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan Television (PTV) was the first TV channel in Pakistan, launched in 1964.

              It was a state-owned channel aimed at education, information, and entertainment.

              45

              In which year was Pakistan’s first nuclear reactor established?

              Correct Answer: 1965
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan’s first nuclear reactor PARR-I (Pakistan Atomic Research Reactor-I) was established in 1965.

              It was set up in Nilore, Islamabad.

              It was mainly for research and training purposes.

              46

              Who was the Longest serving Prime Minister of Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Liaquat Ali Khan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Longest single consecutive tenure belongs to Pakistan's first Prime Minister, Liaquat Ali Khan, who served for 1,524 days from 1947 to 1951

              Yousaf Raza Gillani has become the second-longest-serving prime minister with 1,494 days in power.

              Nawaz Sharif of PML (N) has been the longest-serving prime minister for a non-consecutive term (3 times) At approximately 9 years and 215 days.

               

              Source: https://www.dawn.com/news/727891

              47

              The only Pakistan who has served as President of the UN's general assembly is?

              Correct Answer: Sir Muhammad Zafrulla Khan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Sir Muhammad Zafarullah Khan was elected President of the General Assembly 

              Term: from 1962–1963

              Preceded by: Mongi Slim

              Succeeded by: Carlos Sosa Rodríguez

              He also served as the first Foreign Minister of Pakistan.


              ND14-7-2023

              48

              Who was the first viceroy of India?

              Correct Answer: Lord Canning
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • First Viceroy of India: Lord Canning in 1858
              • Last Viceroy of India: Lord Mountbatten in 1947
              • First Governor of Bengal: Robert Clive
              • First Governor-General of Bengal: Warren Hastings (1772-1785)
              • First Governor-General of India: William Bentinck in 1833

              49

              Sabakzai Dam is being constructed under WAPDA vision 2025 in?

              Correct Answer: Balochistan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Sabakzai Dam is located in Balochistan, Pakistan, near the city of Zhob

              Construction began in 2004

              It was inaugurated by President Pervez Musharraf on 3 September 2007. 

              50

              Furakha dam on river ganges is disputed between?

              Correct Answer: India and Bangladesh
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Farakka Dam on the Ganges (Padma in Bangladesh)

              Itis disputed primarily between India and Bangladesh

              It is a barrage across the Ganga River

              Located in the Murshidabad district in the Indian state of West Bengal

              Construction began in 1961

              Opening date in 1972

              It became operational on 21 April 1975.

              The barrage is about 2,304 metres (7,559 ft) long.


              51

              Which dam is constructed by India under the violation of Indus waters Treaty?

              Correct Answer: Baglihar Dam
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              - The dam is called Baglihar Dam.

              - It is built on the Chenab River, which is one of the major tributaries of the Indus River.

              - India started the construction in the early 2000s.

              It is also known as the Baglihar Hydroelectric Power Project,

              It is a run-of-the-river power project on the Chenab River in Baglihar road in the Ramban district of Jammu and Kashmir, India

              52

              The book KHAKI SHADOWS is written by?

              Correct Answer: K. M. Arif
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              "KHAKI SHADOWS" is written by General Khalid Mahmud Arif.

              Originally published: 2001

              This book describes the powerful role of the army in the politics of Pakistan from its earliest years through the late 1990s.

              53

              Which general designed Pakistan’s military strategy for the 1965 war?

              Correct Answer: General Akhtar Malik
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              General Akhtar Malik was credited with planning and designing Pakistan’s military strategy during the 1965 India-Pakistan war.

              He played a key role in Operation Gibraltar, the plan to infiltrate forces into Indian-administered Kashmir.
              54

              Who was the First Muslim Commander in Chief (C-in-C) of Pakistan Navy?

              Correct Answer: Admiral Muhammad Siddique
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Mohammad Siddiq Choudri, was a three-star rank admiral in the Pakistan Navy who was the first native Commander in Chief of Pakistan Navy.


              Naval Commanders-in-Chief, Pakistan Navy
              1               James Wilfred Jefford 15 August 1947 - 30 January 1953
              2               Haji Mohammad Siddiq Choudri 31 January 1953 - 28 February 1959
              3               Afzal Rahman Khan 1 March 1959 - 20 October 1966
              4               Syed Mohammad Ahsan 20 October 1966 - 31 August 1969
              5               Muzaffar Hassan (last) 1 September 1969 22 December 1971
              ************
              Chiefs of the Naval Staff (              CNS) of Pakistan Navy 17
              First Hasan Hafeez Ahmed (3 March 1972 - 8 March 1975)

              55

              Deepest port of Pakistan is _______?

              Correct Answer: Gwadar
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan purchased Gwadar port (Balochistan) from Oman on 8th September 1958.

              Gwadar officially became part of Pakistan on 8th December 1958.

              *******

              56

              Name the first Pakistani bank which started its operation on 1947?

              Correct Answer: HBL
              Show explanation
              Explanation

               Habib Bank of Pakistan started its operation on August 17, 1947

              The State Bank of Pakistan was inaugurated by Muhammad Ali Jinnah on 01 July 1948.

              ***

              Habib Bank Limited is a Pakistani multinational bank.

              HBL was founded in 1940 by the Habib family.

              HBL became Pakistan's first commercial bank.

              In 1951 it opened its first international branch in Colombo, Sri Lanka.

              The Government nationalized the bank in 1974 and privatized it in 2003.

              In 2003 the Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development acquired a controlling share.

              HBL major shareholding lies with the Aga Khan Fund for Economic Development (AKFED) which is based in Geneva, Switzerland.

               

              As of 2018, HBL has 1700+ branches with presence in over 25 countries spanning four continents.

              57

              Who was the first Woman Prime Minister of Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Benazir Bhutto
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Benazir Bhutto

              • Born: June 21, 1953, in Karachi.
              • Assassinated: December 27, 2007, in Rawalpindi.
              • Spouse: Asif Ali Zardari (1987–2007).
              • Party: Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP).
              • Children: Bilawal Bhutto Zardari, Bakhtawar Bhutto Zardari, Aseefa Bhutto Zardari.
              • Education: Lady Margaret Hall (1973–1977).

              Political Career:

              • Sworn in as First Woman Prime Minister of Pakistan on December 2, 1988.
              • First term as Prime Minister: 1988 to 1990.
              • Second term as Prime Minister: 1993 to 1996.
              • Farooq Laghari dissolved her second government.
              • Served as the 11th and 13th Prime Minister of Pakistan.

              Education Details:

              • Nursery School: Lady Jennings Nursery School in Karachi.
              • College: Radcliffe College
              • University: University of Oxford
              58

              After sui which of the following is the second largest gas field in Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Mari
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Mari gas field, located in District Ghotki, Sindh

              It is the second largest gas field in Pakistan after the Sui gas field.

              Discovered in 1957

              Founded: 1984

              Headquarters: Islamabad


              Additional information:

              Mari Petroleum Company Limited (MPCL) is a Pakistani petroleum exploration and production company based in Islamabad, Pakistan

              59

              What was the magnitude of earthquake as a special representative of UNO’s earthquake relief?

              Correct Answer: 7.6
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The significant earthquake that prompted major UNO relief efforts, particularly in 2005 in Pakistan/Kashmi

              Magnitude: 7.6.

              Date: October 8, 2005.

              Occurred at 08:50:39 Pakistan Standard Time 

              More than 80,000 people perished as a result of the quake,

              60

              Ghulam Muhammad Barrage is also called?

              Correct Answer: Kotri Barrage
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              Kotri Barrage, also known as the Ghulam Muhammad Barrage.
              It is a barrage on the Indus River between Jamshoro and Hyderabad in Sindh, Pakistan.
              The barrage was completed in 1955
              Inaugurated by Ghulam Muhammad Khan.
              Length 1,600 m (5,200 ft)
              It has a discharge capacity of 24,800 cubic metres per second (875,000 cu ft/s).
              It is a gate-controlled weir type barrage with a navigation lock. 
              61

              Khanki Headwork is located on which river?

              Correct Answer: Chenab
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Khanki Headworks is  situated on the River Chenab in Gujranwala District 

              The construction was completed in 1889 (first build)

              It is also used to divert water to the Lower Chenab Canal

              The old "Head Khanki Barrage" was built from 1892 to 1898 on the Chenab River 

              It is one of the oldest headworks in Pakistan

              It was replaced by the modern New Khanki Barrage, which was completed in 2017 (latest build) to enhance flood control and water management. 

              62

              In April 2025, how many Pakistani nationals were killed in southeastern Iran?

              Correct Answer: 8
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              In April 2025, eight Pakistani nationals were killed in southeastern Iran's Sistan-Baluchestan province. 

              The men, who worked as car repair technicians, were shot dead by unidentified gunmen in Mehrestan County on April 12, 2025.

              The attack was later claimed by the Baloch National Army (BNA), a separatist group operating in the region.

              63

              Which militant group claimed responsibility for the November 2024 Quetta railway station bombing?

              Correct Answer: Balochistan Liberation Army
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              On November 9, 2024, a deadly suicide bombing occurred at the Quetta railway station in Balochistan, Pakistan.

              Killing at least 32 people and injuring over 60 others.

              The Balochistan Liberation Army (BLA), an ethnic secessionist militant group, claimed responsibility for the attack.

              64

              What is the main criticism against the Green Pakistan Initiative by environmentalists and Sindh residents?

              Correct Answer: It threatens the Indus Delta ecosystem
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Critics argue the Green Pakistan Initiative’s canal projects divert water from the Indus River, worsening water shortages in Sindh and harming the fragile Indus Delta environment.

              Environmentalists warn this diversion could lead to ecosystem degradation, mangrove loss, and reduced freshwater flows, impacting agriculture and coastal habitats.

              65

              Which Pakistani city hosted the opening ceremony of the 2025 ICC Champions Trophy?

              Correct Answer: Karachi
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Know about ICC Champions Trophy 2025

              Edition: 9th

              Venues: Pakistan/United Arab Emirates

              Participating teams: 8

              Total Matches: 15

              Starting date: 19 Feb 2025

              Closing date: 09 March 2025

              Administrator: International Cricket Council (ICC)

              ****

              66

              What is the name of the indigenously developed weapon system successfully flight-tested by the Pakistan Air Force (PAF) in 2026?

              Correct Answer: Taimoor
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan Air Force (PAF) successfully conducted the flight test of the indigenously developed “Taimoor” weapon system.

              Conducted on 3 January 2026.

              Taimoor Air-Launched Cruise Missile is capable of engaging enemy land and sea targets with high precision at a range of 600 kilometers (372.8 miles).

               

              Additional Information:

              The Taimoor was developed by the Air Weapons Complex of Pakistan.

               ****

              67

              Which is the biggest university of Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Punjab University, Lahore
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The largest university in Pakistan is Punjab University.

              It was established in 1882.

              It is the oldest higher learning institution in the country.

              The University of Agriculture, Faisalabad (UAF) is the largest in terms of area, with a campus spanning 2,550 acres. 

              68

              The term 'Barani' in Pakistani agriculture refers to land that is _____?

              Correct Answer: Dependent entirely on rainfall
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Barani land refers to agricultural land in Pakistan that is entirely dependent on rainfall for cultivation.

              It is common in arid and semi-arid regions where irrigation facilities are limited.

              Crop yields on Barani land vary greatly depending on seasonal rainfall.

              69

              The only river of the Indus Basin Treaty system that is designated for the exclusive use of Pakistan (excluding the Western Rivers) is the ____?

              Correct Answer: Chenab
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Under the Indus Waters Treaty of 1960, the waters of the six main rivers of the Indus Basin were divided between India and Pakistan:

              Western Rivers (Pakistan's Share): The treaty assigned the Indus, Jhelum, and Chenab to Pakistan for unrestricted use (with limited exceptions for India for power generation and irrigation).

              Eastern Rivers (India's Share): The Sutlej, Beas, and Ravi were assigned to India for exclusive use.

              70

              Who was the last Governor General of Pakistan?

              Correct Answer: Iskander Mirza
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              NamePortraitEntered officeLeft officeDate of Birth and DeathPolitical party
              1Muhammad Ali Jinnah15 August 194711 September 194825 December 1876 – 11 September 1948Muslim League
              2Sir Khawaja Nazimuddin
              		14 September 194817 October 195119 July 1894 - 22 October 1964Muslim League
              3Ghulam Mohammad17 October 19516 October 195520 April 1895 - 12 September 1956
              4Iskander Mirza
              		6 October 195523 March 195613 November 1899 - 12 November 1969

              Republican Party


              71

              Which of the following is known as the creator of the fictional puppet character 'Uncle Sargam' introduced in 1976?

              Correct Answer: Farooq Qaiser
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              • Farooq Haider was known as the creator of the fictional puppet character Uncle Sargam introduced in 1976 in children's television show Kaliyan.
              • Farooq was also a cartoonist, newspaper columnist and wrote for the newspaper Daily Nai Baat in Lahore and under the pen name "Meethay Karelay" (English: "Sweet Bitter gourd").
              72

              The term 'WAPDA' (Water and Power Development Authority) was established primarily to manage which two critical sectors?

              Correct Answer: Water and Electric Power
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              WAPDA (Water and Power Development Authority) was established to manage water resources and electricity generation in Pakistan.

              It oversees dams, power plants, and irrigation projects.

              WAPDA plays a key role in national development and flood management.

              73

              The 'Khewra Salt Mine', one of the oldest and largest in the world, is located in the foothills of which mountain range?

              Correct Answer: Salt Range
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Khewra Salt Mine is also known as the Mayo Salt Mine.

              It is located in the Salt Range mountains in the Jhelum District of Punjab, Pakistan.

              This low mountain system extends approximately 300 kilometers between the Jhelum and Indus rivers.

              It remains the world's second-largest salt mine and Pakistan's largest and oldest.

              The mine features unique structures made entirely of salt bricks, including a replica of the Badshahi Mosque.

              It is a salt hospital for asthma patients, and artistic carvings like a model of the Great Wall of China.

              74

              The oldest mountain range in Pakistan, located in Sindh and Balochistan, is the _____?

              Correct Answer: Kirthar Range
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Kirthar Range is a significant mountain range in southern Pakistan.

              It marks the boundary between the provinces of Sindh and Balochistan.

              It extends for approximately 300 kilometers (190 miles) from the Mula River southward to Cape Monze on the Arabian Sea. 

              75

              Redcliff award was given in which date and time?

              Correct Answer: 17th August 1947 5:00 PM
              Show explanation
              Explanation
              The Radcliffe Line demarcated the boundary between India and Pakistan, including East Pakistan (now Bangladesh).
              It was officially announced on 17 August 1947, two days after India’s independence and three days after Pakistan’s independence.
              The line was drawn by Sir Cyril Radcliffe, who had never visited India before being tasked with this responsibility.
              76

              The main economic term for the practice of leasing agricultural land for a share of the crop, common in parts of Pakistan, is _____?

              Correct Answer: Muzara’ah (Sharecropping)
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Muzara’ah is an agricultural system where land is leased in return for a share of the crop.

              It allows farmers without land to cultivate and share the produce with the landowner.

              This practice is common in rural Pakistan and helps manage land and labor resources efficiently.

              77

              The 'Government of India Act 1935' introduced the system of 'Diarchy' at the provincial level, which meant the division of _____?

              Correct Answer: Administrative powers between British and Indian officials
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Government of India Act 1935 introduced Diarchy at the provincial level, dividing administrative powers between the British authorities and Indian ministers.

              Certain departments like finance and law were reserved for the British, while others were transferred to Indian ministers.

              This system aimed to gradually involve Indians in governance but retained ultimate control with the British.

              78

              Who among the following was the commander of Muhammad Ghori and also founder of the Slave Dynasty in the sub-continent?

              Correct Answer: Qutab ud Din Aibak
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Qutab ud Din Aibak was the first ruler of the Muslim dynasty in Sub-Continent

              ......

              Qutb al-Din Aibak  was a general of Muhammad Ghori.

              Qutab ud Din Aibak was in charge of the Ghurid territories in northern India, and after Muhammad Ghori's death.

              he became the ruler of an independent kingdom that evolved into the Delhi Sultanate ruled by the Mamluk dynasty.

              Qutab ud Din Aibak is buried in Lahore. 

              ....

              Al-khafif was the first ruler of soomra dynasty (1011-1026)

              Mughal emperor Akbar the first Muslim ruler who tried to reconcile the differences between Muslims and Hindus

              Razia Sultana the first woman ruler of India

              79

              The major wheat producing region in Pakistan is :

              Correct Answer: Punjab
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Punjab being the second largest by area

              Most agricultural province of Pakistan

              Contributes almost 75% to the wheat production

              There are two zones of the Punjab: lower and upper.


              80

              The term "zamindar" in the context of agriculture in Pakistan refers to :

              Correct Answer: Landowner
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Zamindari System was introduced by Lord Cornwallis in 1793.

              It was introduced in the provinces of:

              • Bengal
              • Bihar
              • Orissa
              • Varanasi.

              81

              What percentage of Pakistan workforce is employed in the agricultural sector ?

              Correct Answer: Approximately 40 %
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Pakistan's agriculture sector contributes around 23 percent of GDP

              Employs 37.4 percent of the national labour force.

              70 percent of Pakistan's exports are directly or indirectly derived from agriculture.


              82

              Which crop is known as the "king of crops " and is a staple food in Pakistan ?

              Correct Answer: Wheat
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Wheat is a staple food crop of Pakistan

              dominating all crops in acreage and production.

              Mainly grown under irrigated conditions

              Which of following is the major oilseed crop in Pakistan ?

              Oilseed

              84

              The livestock sector in Pakistan includes the farming of

              Correct Answer: All of above
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The livestock sector in Pakistan includes the farming of Cattle,poultry and Fish

              Livestock farming is an important resource for Pakistan 

              Pakistan's main livestock consists of buffalo, cattle, sheep, goats, camels, horses, and chickens.

              These animals provide hides and skins, meat, eggs, and milk for the country

              Valuable source of trade internationally.


              85

              The cultivation of which crop is a major source of income for the rural population in the Swat valley

              Correct Answer: Maize
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The cultivation of Maize  is a major source of income for the rural population in the Swat valley 

              The economy of district Swat is predominantly based on agriculture

              which is main source of livelihood for local population.

              The major crops cultivated in Swat include rice, onion, wheat, maize, tomato and vegetables.


              86

              Which of following is a key factor affecting the productivity of agriculture in Pakistan

              Correct Answer: Water Scacity
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Agriculture is considered the backbone of Pakistan's economy

              Pakistan's principal natural resources are arable land and water.

              Agriculture accounts for about 18.9% of Pakistan's GDP

              The most agricultural province is Punjab


              87

              The cultivation of which crop is a significant contributor to the economy of the thar desert region in Pakistan ?

              Correct Answer: pulses
              Show explanation
              Explanation

               Pulses are widely grown in the Thar Desert.

              The canal also provides drinking water.

              The Thar Desert is located in India and Pakistan.

              85% of the Thar is located within India

              Remaining 15% is located in Pakistan.



              88

              which of the following is a high value cash crop often cultivated in the Swat valley of KPK?

              Correct Answer: Wheat
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Swat valley is locally known as Pakistan's Switzerland

              Swat valley is known as “The Mini Switzerland”

              nearly 37% of Swat's economy relies on tourism

              The major crops cultivated in Swat include wheat, maize, tomato and vegetables.



              89

              The term "Kisan Package " in Pakistan is associated with

              Correct Answer: Agricultural subsidies
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              The Kissan Package is aimed to help with the rehabilitation and economic recovery of the flood-affected farmers,

              who will be able to benefit from a 0% mark-up subsidy on the loan for up to 6 months.


              90

              Which crop is known as the golden fiber and is major cash crop in Pakistan

              Correct Answer: Jute
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Jute was also known as the ‘Golden Fibre’.

              mainly because of its colour.

              It grows well on alluvial soil

              Requires high temperature, heavy rainfall, and humid climate.

              This crop is grown in the tropical areas.

              India and Bangladesh are the leading producers of jute.



              91

              The best Variety of Apples is grown in which province of Pakistan ?

              Correct Answer: Baluchistan
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Baluchistan is the key contributor of Apple production

              While KPK stands on second number

              with contributing 25 percent of national Apple growth.

               Baluchistan is called land of Apples.


              92

              Which is the most widely used irrigation method in Pakistan______?

              Correct Answer: Perennial Canals
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Perennial Canals flow throughout the year

              Thus referred to as perennial canals.

              They draw water from perennial rivers or from artificial reservoirs

              which sustain a high water level on the upstream side.

              93

              Which is the main source of agriculture credit ?

              Correct Answer: Zarai Taraqiati Bank
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Modern agriculture technology machinery and Agriculture loans in Pakistan.

              94

              Which of the following is a Rabi Crop?

              Correct Answer: Mustard
              Show explanation
              Explanation

              Mustard is a Rabi crop.

              Rabi crop is grown in autumn or early winter

              They harvested in spring or early summer

              They are grown in October or November. 

              major rabi crop in India and Pakistan is:

              • wheat, 
              • barley,
              • mustard,
              • sesame,
              •  peas.

              Rabi crops are known as winter crops.

              Banana fruit is classifies in the which category?

              Show explanation
              Explanation

                • Soft, fleshy, and contains multiple seeds (small or undeveloped in cultivated varieties).
                • Edible and rich in carbohydrates and vitamins.
                • Commonly grown in tropical and subtropical regions.

                The Indus Basin irrigation system, one of the largest in the world primarily serves agriculture in which region of Pakistan?

                Correct Answer: Southern Punjab
                Show explanation

                The Sukkur Barrage, is the pride of Pakistan's irrigation system

                In Pakistan cultivation of Basmati rice is particularly associated in which region

                Correct Answer: Gujranwala

                ice is an important Kharif crop.

                103

                Which financial ratio indicates the proportion of revenue that exceeds the COGS?

                Correct Answer: Grass profit margin
                Show explanation
                Explanation

                The financial ratio that indicates the proportion of revenue is the gross profit margin.

                The gross profit margin is calculated by:

                  • subtracting the COGS from the revenue
                  • and then dividing the result by the revenue.

                  This ratio represents the percentage of revenue that remains as:
                  gross profit after accounting for the cost of producing the goods or services.

                  Pakistan won ACC Under-19 Asia Cup 2025 by defeating which country's team in the final?

                  Correct Answer: India
                  Show explanation

                  About ACC Under-19 Asia Cup 2025

                  Edition: 12th

                  Dates: 12 to 21 December 2025

                  Venue: Dubai, United Arab Emirates (UAE)

                  Champions: Pakistan (2nd title)

                  Runners-up: India

                  Participants: 8 teams

                  Matches: 15

                  Player of the series: Sameer Minhas (Pak)

                  Most runs: Pakistani Sameer Minhas (471)

                   

                  Additional Information:

                  Bangladesh won the last edition after defeating India by 59 runs in the final of the 2024 edition.

                  105

                  Which medal awarded to Field Marshal Asim Munir in 2025 for his efforts to advance Pak-Saudi ties?

                  Correct Answer: King Abdulaziz Medal of Excellent Class
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  On 22 December 2025, Saudi Arabia’s Defence Minister Prince Khalid bin Salman bin Abdulaziz conferred the King Abdulaziz Medal of Excellent Class on Chief of Defence Forces (CDF) and Chief of Army Staff Field Marshal Asim Munir for his efforts to “advance Pak-Saudi ties”.

                  Munir was awarded during his visit to Saudi Arabia.

                  This medal is usually awarded to those who have rendered outstanding services to Saudi Arabia or one of its institutions.

                  Key crops include wheat, rice, cotton, and sugarcane.
