                  110

                  What is the area of KPK?

                  Correct Answer: 101,741 sq.km,
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  KPK Established in 1 July 1970

                  KPK Renamed in 19 April 2010

                  FATA Merged in 31 May 2018

                  KPK Capital and largest city is Peshawar

                  Total Area 101,741 sq.km,

                  Located in the northwestern region

                  KPK has 36 districts and 8th divisions.

                  Khyber Pakhtunkhwa is the fourth-largest province of Pakistan by land area

                  and the third-largest province by population. 

                  *********

                  The total area of Pakistan is 881,913 km² 

                  while

                  140,914 sq Km, is the total area of Sindh Province

                  13,297 km² is the total area of Azad Kashmir

                  347,190 sq Km is the total area of the Balochistan Province

                  74,521 Sq Km, is the total area of Khyber Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KPK)

                  205,344 sq Km, is the total area of the Punjab Province.

                  SH6-9-2023


                  111

                  The Ravi River falls in which river ?

                  Correct Answer: Chenab
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Ravi River, one of Punjab's five legendary rivers

                  Originates in India's Himalayas,

                  Flows along the India-Pakistan border, passes by Lahore,

                  And then empties into the Chenab River

                  Confluence: Joins the Chenab River south of Ahmadpur Sial, Pakistan.

                  Tributary of: The Indus River. 

                  112

                  If USA increased tariffs, Pakistan did?

                  Correct Answer: Ignored
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Pakistan's official response to the increased U.S. tariffs was not to impose retaliatory tariffs but rather to engage in diplomatic negotiation and explore alternative strategies.

                  113

                  Gawadar Sea Port is in which sea?

                  Correct Answer: Arabian sea
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Gwadar Port is situated on the shores of the Arabian Sea in the city of Gwadar, located in Balochistan.

                  Pakistan has three big sea ports named as Karachi Port, Muhammad Bin Qasim port, and Gwadar port.

                  114

                  Sulaiman Range is famous for?

                  Correct Answer: Takht-e-Sulaiman
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Sulaiman Range in Pakistan is famous for Takht-i-Sulaima

                  It is famous for the extension of the Hindu Kush mountains, with its highest peak, Takht-i-Sulaima

                   Elevation 3,487 m.

                  Sulaiman Mountains First ascender George White

                  Takhat-e-Sulaiman peak is located in Balochistan.

                  Its peak is located on the border of both the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa and Balochistan 

                  115

                  Pakistan Stock Exchange Name?

                  Correct Answer: PSX
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                   Pakistan Stock Exchange is officially abbreviated as PSX

                  Pakistan Stock Exchange Limited, commonly known by its acronym PSX.

                  After the merger of Karachi, Lahore, and Islamabad stock exchanges in 2016.

                  Karachi Stock Exchange (now part of Pakistan Stock Exchange) was the largest and most active in the country.

                  Founded: As Karachi Stock Exchange (KSE) in 1947; integrated into PSX in 2016.

                  Headquarters: Karachi, Pakistan. 


                  116

                  PCCC in agriculture stands for?

                  Correct Answer: Pakistan Central Cotton Committee
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Pakistan Central Cotton Committee (PCCC)

                  It was established in 1948 by an Act of Parliament to boost Pakistan's cotton sector through research, development, and improved practices

                  117

                  Karachi-Chaman motorway is called?

                  Correct Answer: N-5
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Karachi-Chaman route primarily follows the N-25 National Highway

                  It is also known as the RCD Highway,

                  A crucial link to Afghanistan, often called the "killer highway"

                  It is a 813 km national highway in Pakistan is a single-lane highway

                  New Project Name: Balochistan Expressway (under development as an upgraded motorway).

                  118

                  Neelum–Jhelum Hydroelectric Power Project is located in which district?

                  Correct Answer: District Muzaffarabad
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation


                  The power station is located 42 km (26 mi) south of Muzaffarabad,

                  It has an installed capacity of 969 MW

                  Designed to divert water from the Neelum River to a power station on the Jhelum River. 


                  HISTORY 
                  On 7 July 2007, the China consortium, comprising CGGC-CMEC (Gezhouba Group and China National Machinery Import and Export Corporation), was awarded a contract to construct the dam and power station. 

                  Construction began in 2008

                  Opening date 14 August 2018

                  Height 60 m (197 ft)

                  Length 125 m (410 ft)

                  119

                  According to Population, Pakistan is ________ largest country in the world?

                  Correct Answer: 5th
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Pakistan is the world's fifth-most populous country.

                  The population density in Pakistan is 287 per Km2 (742 people per mi2).

                  Pakistan world's second-largest Muslim population.

                  the top 10 most populous countries in the world

                  1. India
                  2. China
                  3. the United States
                  4. Indonesia
                  5. Pakistan
                  6. Brazil
                  7. Nigeria
                  8. Bangladesh
                  9. Russia
                  10.  Mexico
                  120

                  Which fort of Sindh is also known as “The Great Wall Of Sindh”?

                  Correct Answer: Ranikot Fort
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The fort of Sindh, known as the “Great Wall of Sind,h” is Ranikot Fort

                  It is a 19th-century (reconstructed) Talpur-era fort near Sann in Jamshoro District, Sindh, Pakistan.

                  The fort's ramparts have been compared to the Great Wall of China.

                  The site was nominated in 1993 by the Pakistan National Commission for UNESCO world heritage status. 

                  Length: 32 kilometer,19.88 (mi)

                  Location: Jamshoro district, Sindh

                  121

                  Pakistan launched an operation in Kashmir in 1965 which was called operation?

                  Correct Answer: Gibraltar
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  The Prelude to September 6, 1965 is called Operation Gibraltar.

                  Tensions had been escalating since August 1965, when Pakistan embarked on an undeclared war in Jammu and Kashmir.

                  This began with Operation Gibraltar, a covert mission designed to infiltrate Indian-administered Kashmir and incite a rebellion against Indian rule.

                  122

                  What is Malakhra?

                  Correct Answer: A Game
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Malakhra is a traditional Sindhi wrestling game popular in Pakistan.

                  It involves wrestlers attempting to unbalance each other using a cloth tied to their opponent's waist.

                  A traditional wrestling match is played annually at the Urs of Lal Shahbaz Qalandar at Sehwan Sharif.

                  123

                  Kirthar Hill is located between ______?

                  Correct Answer: Sindh and Balochistan
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation
                  • The elevation of the Kirthar Mountains is 2,260 meters (7,410 feet).
                    • The highest peak in the Kirthar Range is Zardak Peak.
                    • It stands at 7,430 feet (2,260 meters).
                    • The Kirthar Range is situated along the border of Balochistan and Sindh provinces.
                    • The hill station of Gorakh, located in the Kirthar Mountains, is situated off Dadu in Sindh.
                    124

                    The "Salt Range " is located in?

                    Correct Answer: Punjab
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The highest peak of salt range is Soan Sakaser

                    The Salt Range is a mountain range in the north of the Punjab province of Pakistan

                    The range extends along the south of the Potohar Plateau and the north of the Jhelum River.

                    The salt range is situated between rivers Soan and River Jhelum

                    Sakesar (Khushab District) is the highest mountain in the salt region
                    675 meters is the average height of the salt range

                    The Upper Indus plain area is located in the South of the Salt Range and east of Sulemania

                    ******

                    ND17-7-2023

                    125

                    Who is teh 14th President of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Asif Ali Zardari
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Asif Ali Zardari is the current president of Pakistan since March 2024

                    Number: 14th president 

                    Predecessor: Dr. Arif Alvi

                    Party affiliation: PPP

                    Elected in March 2024

                    126

                    Which tectonic plates meet near Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Indian and Eurasian
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Pakistan sits at the collision zone of the Indian Plate, the Eurasian Plate, and the Arabian Plate, 

                    Indian Plate: Pushing northward into the Eurasian Plate.

                    Eurasian Plate: The large continental plate that the Indian Plate is colliding with.

                    Arabian Plate: Subducting beneath the Eurasian Plate in the southwest, forming the Makran arc and influencing boundaries near Karachi. 

                    127

                    Which of the following is located in the south of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Arabian Sea
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Arabian Sea lies in the South of Pakistan

                    It is located south of Pakistan with a coastline of 1,046 kilometers.

                    Indus River Falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi

                    Indus River flows into the Arabian Sea called the Indus Delta 

                    Indus Delta Designated on 5 November 2002

                    128

                    How many Ministers are there in Cabinet Mission?

                    Correct Answer: 3
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The following three Cabinet Ministers of England included:

                    1. Sir Pethick Lawrence, Secretary of State for India.
                    2. Sir Stafford Cripps, President of the Board of Trade,
                    3. Alexander, the First Lord of the Admiralty

                    The Cabinet Mission took place in 1946.

                    The main objective of the Cabinet Mission was to find ways and means of peaceful transfer of power in India.

                    The Cabinet Mission consisted of a few members symbolizing the desire for Indian unity.

                    129

                    Who was the First Slave King of Delhi Sultanate?

                    Correct Answer: Qutubuddin Aibak
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Qutab ud Din Aibak was the first ruler of the Muslim dynasty in Sub-Continent.

                    ......

                    Qutb al-Din Aibak was a general of Muhammad Ghori.

                    Qutab ud Din Aibak was in charge of the Ghurid territories in northern India, and after Muhammad Ghori's death.

                    He became the ruler of an independent kingdom that evolved into the Delhi Sultanate ruled by the Mamluk dynasty.

                    Qutab ud Din Aibak is buried in Lahore. 

                    ....

                    Al-khafif was the first ruler of soomra dynasty (1011-1026).

                    Mughal emperor Akbar the first Muslim ruler who tried to reconcile the differences between Muslims and Hindus.

                    Razia Sultana the first woman ruler of India.

                    130

                    Pothohar Plateau is famous for _____?

                    Correct Answer: Salt deposits
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    The Pothohar Plateau also known as Pothwar, is a plateau in north-eastern Pakistan

                    The Pothohar plateau is rich in minerals like gypsum, limestone, coal, oil, and rock salt.

                    Located between the Indus River and the Jhelum River
                    ******

                    • The Potwar Plateau includes parts of the current four districts:

                      Jhelum

                      Chakwal

                      Rawalpindi

                      Attock

                      ****

                      The Pothohar plateau is rich in minerals like gypsum, limestone, coal, oil, and rock salt.

                      Potohar Plateau سطح مرتفع پوٹھوہار .consists of:

                      • District Rawalpindi District
                      • Chakwal District
                      • Jhelum District
                      • Khushab District,
                      • Mirpur District
                      • Islamabad
                      • Mianwali District,
                      • Sargodha District (Northern Belt)
                    131

                    Which is the province of Pakistan that shares the longest border?

                    Correct Answer: Balochistan
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Balochistan shares an international border of about 1,400 km, mainly with Iran and Afghanistan.

                    It is Pakistan’s largest province by area.

                    It covering roughly 44% of the country’s total land area.

                    132

                    Which country accepted Pakistan first?

                    Correct Answer: Iran
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation


                    Iran was the first country to recognize Pakistan as an independent state in 1947


                    They officially accepted Pakistan after Indonesia.


                    the Cold War (1945–1991), both countries were part of the Western Bloc against the Eastern Bloc. 

                    Iran aided Pakistan in the Indo-Pakistani War of 1965 and Indo-Pakistani War of 1971


                    Additional Information


                    The United States was the first non-Muslim country to recognize Pakistan 


                    France was the first country where Pakistan's flag was raised.


                    Pakistan is the only country in the world that does not recognize Armenia as a state

                    ******

                    ND27122022 ND19-3-2023ND23-4-2023



                    133

                    Chaudhry Rehmat Ali first used the word 'Pakistan' in his pamphlet 'now or never' in which year?

                    Correct Answer: 1933
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The word was first introduced now or never in 1933 by Chaudhry Rahmat Ali.

                    The "Pakistan Declaration" titled Now or Never means Are We to Live or Perish Forever?

                    In this pamphlet, the word Pakistan was used for the first time 

                    This pamphlet was derived from the two-nation theory of Muslim reformer Syed Ahmed Khan

                    it was a pamphlet, referring to the names of the five northern regions of British India: 

                    1. Punjab
                    2. Afghanistan
                    3. Kashmir
                    4. Sindh
                    5. Baluchistan. (Pakistan)

                    ***


                    134

                    The first nuclear power plant in Pakistan was established at ____?

                    Correct Answer: Karachi
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The first nuclear power plant was established in Karachi, 
                    • With the help of Canada 
                    • KANUPP is Pakistan's first nuclear power plant,
                    • Inaugurated on November 28, 1972,
                    • Total gross capacity of 137 MW.

                    while

                    K2 & K3 with the help of China 

                    K2  started on 21 May 2021 and has an 1100 MW capacity.

                    K3 is expected to be started in 2022.


                    135

                    Rowlatt act was passed in _____?

                    Correct Answer: 1919
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The Rowlatt Satyagraha was a national protest against Rowlatt Act introduced in 1919.
                    • The Rowlatt Act was passed on 6th February 1919.
                    • Rowlatt Satyagraha was founded in 1919 by Mahatma Gandhi.
                    • Gandhiji became a true national leader as a result of the Rowlatt Satyagraha.
                    • The Rowlatt Act gave the British government the ability to arrest and imprison anyone for no cause.
                    • The Rowlatt Act was officially known as the "anarchical and revolutionary offenses act."
                    • Gandhiji referred to the Rowlatt Act as a "black act."
                    • On April 6, 1919, Mahatma Gandhi resolved to fight the Rowlatt Act and issued a call for satyagraha.
                    • The British viceroy who approved the Rowlatt Act was Lord Chelmsford.
                    136

                    Shahzia Sikander is a Pakistani American is?

                    Correct Answer: Visual artist
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Shahzia Sikander (born 1969) is a Pakistani-American visual artist. 

                    Born: 1969Lahore

                    Alma mater : National College of Arts, Lahore, Rhode Island School of Design

                    Known for: Visual Art, Contemporary art

                    Awards: MacArthur Fellowship

                    137

                    Who was awarded Nishan-e-Haider in the Kargil War?

                    Correct Answer: Sher Khan
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    During the 1999 Kargil War, two Pakistani soldiers were awarded the Nishan-e-Haider (Pakistan's highest military honor):

                    Captain Karnal Sher Khan

                    Havildar Lalak Jan

                    138

                    Nawa-e-Waqt started as?

                    Correct Answer: Weekly
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Nawa-e-Waqt was launched as a weekly newspaper and was later converted into a daily

                    Nawaiwaqt was launched on March 23, 1940, under the leadership of Hameed Nizami

                     Nawaiwaqt passionately supported the All-India Muslim League.

                    Nawaiwaqt is an Urdu daily newspaper in Pakistan which is currently owned by Majid Nizami Trust.

                    The headquarter of Nawaiwaqt is in Lahour. 

                    139

                    Muhammad Tughluq shifted his capital from Delhi to?

                    Correct Answer: Daulatabad
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Muhammad bin Tughlaq moved the capital of the Delhi Sultanate from Delhi to Daulatabad (formerly Devagiri) in 1327 AD.

                    Daulatabad, situated in present-day Maharashtra, was given a new name signifying "Abode of Wealth". 

                    140

                    Doab between Ravi and Chenab rivers is called?

                    Correct Answer: Rachna doab
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Rachna doab includes all the area between the Chenab and Ravi Rivers

                    Rachna Doab can be classified as one of the main regions of Punjab, Pakistan.

                    The name was given by the Mughal emperor Akbar

                    ***

                    Five Important Doabs of Pakistan 

                    Sindh Sagar Doab is b/w: Indus & Jhelum rivers 

                    Jech Doab is b/w: Jhelum & Chenab rivers 

                    Bari Doab is b/w: Ravi & Sutlej rivers

                    Rachna Doab  is b/w: Ravi  & Chenab rivers  

                    Bias Doab is b/w: Bias & Sutlej rivers 

                    Doab Chaj is b/w: Chenab and Jhelum.


                    141

                    The largest political party in East Pakistan at the time of separation, was_____.

                    Correct Answer: Awami league
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The largest political party in East Pakistan at the time of separation was the Awami League,

                    Led by Sheikh Mujibur Rahman

                    He secured a landslide victory in the 1970 general elections, won all seats in East Pakistan 

                    1970 Election: The Awami League won 160 out of 300 National Assembly seats, all from East Pakistan, demonstrating overwhelming Bengali support.
                    142

                    Which place is famous for marble in Pakitan?

                    Correct Answer: Swat
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The region of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa has vast marble reserves, and Swat, along with areas like Buner and Mohmand Agency,

                    It is renowned for high-quality varieties such as Badal Grey, Tavera, and the highly demanded Ziarat White marble

                    143

                    Who presented the six points formula?

                    Correct Answer: Shaikh Mujeeb ur Rehman
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Sheikh Mujibur Rahman announced the six points in Lahore.

                    144

                    Which was the official language declared in 1956 constitution?

                    Correct Answer: Both A & B
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Constitution of 1956 · Pakistan was declared as the Islamic Republic

                    while

                    Both Urdu and Bengali were declared as official and national languages of Pakistan in the 1956 constitution

                                All important questions of  "the 1956 constitution"

                                1956 constitution is promulgated on 23 March 1956

                                ***

                                Iskander Mirza abrogated the 1956 constitution

                                ***

                                In 1956 the designation of Governor General changed to President

                                ****

                                40 years was the age prescribed for president in the 1956 constitution

                                **

                                The Constitution of 1956 contained 234 articles divided into thirteen parts and six schedules

                                **

                                The constitution of 1956 remained implemented for 2 years 6 months and 14 days.

                                **

                                The constitution of 1956 provided a Parliamentary form of government for the country.

                                **

                                1956 constitution legislature was Unicameral

                                **

                                The Constitution of 1956 provided for a federal system with the principle of parity between East Pakistan and West Pakistan

                                145

                                World War-I was fought between:

                                Correct Answer: 1914-1918
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                World War I was a global war originating in Europe 

                                This war lasted from 28 July 1914 to 11 November 1918.

                                Chlorine was First used during World War I at Ypres in April 1915

                                After World War 1 influenza disease occurred in 1918

                                During the First World War, Russia signed the Peace Treaty (1917) with Germany.

                                ***

                                World War II or the Second World War, was a global war

                                This war started in 1939 and ended in 1945



                                146

                                When Mohammad Ali Bogra presented Bogra Formula in the assembly?

                                Correct Answer: October 1953
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Bogra Formula was a political compromise presented and proposed by Prime Minister Bogra on 7 October 1953

                                The Bogra Formula sought to permanently abolish the Governor-General and to be replaced with the elected figurehead presidency.

                                Mohammad Ali Bogra was the 2nd Bengali to serve as Prime Minister of Pakistan.

                                Sahibzada Mohammad Ali Bogra was the 3rd Prime Minister of Pakistan from 17 April 1953 till 12 August 1955.

                                He was the 3rd & 7th Minister of Foreign Affairs from 13 June 1962 till 23 January 1963

                                Bogra was Minister of Defence from 17 April 1953 to 24 October 1954.

                                ND5-07-2023


                                147

                                Which Pakistani Scientist was awarded Nobel Prize for physics in 1979?

                                Correct Answer: Dr Abdus Salam
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                In 1979,  Dr. Abdul Salam won the Nobel Prize for physics.

                                He was the second Muslim and first Pakistani scientist to get the Nobel Prize.


                                He won 12 Awards with his wife.

                                1. Copley Medal: 1990
                                2. Royal Medal: 1978
                                3. Hughes Medal: 1964
                                4. Pride of Performance: 1958
                                5. Nishan-e-Imtiaz
                                6. Adams Prize: 1958
                                7. Lomonosov Gold Medal: 1983
                                8. Atoms for Peace Award
                                9. Gold Medal for Outstanding Contributions to Physics: 1981
                                10. Smith's Prize for Essay: 1950
                                11. Jožef Stefan Medal: 1980
                                12. Cristoforo Colombo Prize: 1992

                                Additional information 

                                As of 2018, there have been two Pakistanis who have won the Nobel Prize.

                                • First was Pakistani physicist Abdus Salam
                                • Second was Malala Yousafzai
                                148

                                The most beautiful stone Marble is extracted from which province of Pakistan?

                                Correct Answer: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Stone-Marble is founded in Chilas District of KPK in huge quantities and Federally Administrated Tribal Areas (FATA)

                                KPK produces around 60percent of total Pakistan's marble 

                                Turkmenistan country`s capital is known as the 'White-Marble' City

                                Which of these is not of the five rivers of Punjab?

                                Correct Answer: Kunhar
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Kunhar River is  located in the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa province, northern Pakistan.

                                Its previous name was NainSukh.

                                Length : 166 kilometres 

                                150

                                Karachi faced which climate issue?

                                Correct Answer: Heatwaves
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Karachi often experiences extreme heatwaves, especially during summer months.

                                These heatwaves can cause health hazards, power shortages, and water scarcity.

                                151

                                The oldest mountains of the world are present in India and youngest mountain of the world are present in?

                                Correct Answer: Pakistan
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Aravalli Range, an eroded stub of ancient mountains, is the oldest range of fold mountains in India.

                                 

                                The Himalayan Mountain is the youngest range of Pakistan

                                It's thought that this range formed about 40 million years ago

                                 It is located between Tibetan plateau and Ganga plain
                                152

                                In which province of Pakistan Mirani dam is being constructed?

                                Correct Answer: Balochistan
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Mirani Dam is in Kech District in the Balochistan province of Pakistan.

                                ﻿Mirani Dam is located in the Dashat river



                                Mirani Dam was completed in July 2006 and began impounding the Dasht River in August 2006.


                                The feasibility report of the Mirani dam project was first completed in 1956. 


                                Mirani Dam was commissioned by(WAPDA) in 2001 to provide water for Gwadar city. 

                                The Length of Mirani Dam is 3,350 ft (1,020 m)

                                153

                                How many seats were won by the Awami league in the 1970 election?

                                Correct Answer: 160
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • 160 seats was won by awami league in the 1970 election
                                • ***
                                  • In the elections of 1970, the Awami League won 167 of 169 East Pakistan seats in the National Assembly
                                  • but none of West Pakistan's 138 seats.
                                  • It also won 288 of the 300 provincial assembly seats in East Pakistan.

                                154

                                Who transformed the Muslim League into a Mass Movement?

                                Correct Answer: Muhammad Ali Jinnah
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Muhammad Ali Jinnah turned the Muslim League from an elite political party into a mass-based movement.

                                This transformation was crucial for the creation of Pakistan in 1947.

                                155

                                The capital of the Mughal Empire was shifted from Agra to Delhi by ________?

                                Correct Answer: Shahjehan
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The capital of the Mughal empire was shifted from Agra to Delhi by Shah Jahan in the year of 1638.

                                The new capital was officially named Shahjahanabad in 1648.

                                It is now known as Old Delhi. 

                                The architect behind the Taj Mahal, Ustad Ahmad Lahori, is credited with designing the Red Fort.

                                156

                                Which of the following mineral deposits are found in Potwar Plateau?

                                Correct Answer: All of these
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Potwar Plateau includes parts of the districts of Jhelum, Chakwal and Rawalpindi.

                                The Pothohar plateau is rich in minerals like gypsum, limestone, coal, oil and rock salt.

                                157

                                Which country is in the north west of Pakistan?

                                Correct Answer: Afghanistan
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation


                                158

                                Which dam is not in Punjab? Mangla Dam, Chashma Dam, Rasul Dam, Tarbela Dam

                                Correct Answer: Tarbela Dam
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Chashma Dam (Barrage) is located in the Mianwali District of the Punjab province.

                                Mangla Dam is situated on the Jhelum River and lies partially in the Jhelum District of the Punjab province and partially in the Mirpur District of Azad Kashmir.

                                Rasul Dam (Barrage) is on the River Jhelum, located between the Jhelum District and Mandi Bahauddin District, both in the Punjab province.

                                Tarbela Dam is an earth-fill dam located on the Indus River in the Haripur District of the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KPK) province. 

                                159

                                Which river provides water to Sindh?

                                Correct Answer: Indus River
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                The Indus River is also known as the Sindhu River or Darya-e-Sindh.

                                It is the primary source of water for the Sindh province of Pakistan.

                                Often described as the province's "lifeline".

                                Because it sustains the region's agriculture, economy, and environment. 

                                160

                                Who was honored with Nishan-e-Haider for his bravery in 1965 Indo-Pak War?

                                Correct Answer: Raja Aziz Bhatti
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Raja Aziz Bhatti was awarded the Nishan-e-Haider (Lit.: Emblem of Lion) for his actions of valor during the Battle of Burki in the Indo-Pak War of 1965.

                                Major Raja Aziz Bhatti was the 1st PMA long course holder who also received the Sword of Honor and Norman medal

                                while 

                                Nishan-e-Haider's First award: Muhammad Sarwar

                                Nishan-e-Haider's Last award: Havildar Lalak Jan

                                161

                                Name the palace that is the official residence of the President of Pakistan?

                                Correct Answer: Aiwan-e-Sadr
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Aiwan-e-Sadr is the official residence and workplace of the President of Pakistan.

                                It is located in Islamabad.

                                It is s used for state ceremonies and official meetings.

                                162

                                One Unit was established in West Pakistan in ______?

                                Correct Answer: September 30, 1955
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                On 30 September, 1955

                                Until 1970

                                Idea of Muhamad Ali Bogra ,named Bogra Formula.

                                Implemented by Ch. Muhammad Ali


                                agm.04.03.23


                                163

                                What was the first capital of the Suri Empire founded by Sher Shah Suri?

                                Correct Answer: Sasaram
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                Sher Shah Suri's initial or primary capital was Sasaram (in modern-day Bihar). 

                                He also established the city of Shergarh (previously Humayun's Din Panah, now the Purana Qila area) as a capital during his reign in Delhi.

                                164

                                The Establishment of Delhi Sultanate begin with the invasion of _____?

                                Correct Answer: Muhammad Ghouri
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation

                                In 1192, the Afghan warrior Muhammad Ghori defeated the Rajput Confederacy, led by Prithviraj Chauhan, near Tarain.

                                After Ghori's death in 1206, his generals divided the Ghurid territories and established the Delhi Sultanate.

                                Qutb ud-Din Aibak, a slave soldier and Ghori's army commander, became the new sultan of Delhi. 

                                165

                                The highest official rank of Pakistan Army is ________?

                                Correct Answer: General
                                Show explanation
                                Explanation
                                • Pakistan Army: The highest official rank is Field Marshal, though it is rarely awarded (e.g., General Ayub Khan in 1965). The highest active rank is General.
                                  • Pakistan Navy: The highest rank is Admiral.
                                  • Pakistan Air Force: The highest rank is Air Chief Marshal.
                                  • Police: The highest rank is Inspector General of Police (IGP), overseeing provincial or national law enforcement.
                                  166

                                  CNIC stands for _______?

                                  Correct Answer: Computerized National Identity Card
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation

                                  CNIC Stands for Computerized National Identity Card

                                  The CNIC was introduced in 2000

                                  NADRA Founded: March 10, 2000, Islamabad

                                  NADRA Headquarters: Islamabad
                                  ***

                                  History

                                  Pakistan initiated its National Identity Card System in 1973 under Article 30 of the Second Amendment.

                                  The first NIC being that of Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto, the Prime Minister of the time

                                  167

                                  Who is the highest wicket taker in ODI and Tests for Pakistan?

                                  Correct Answer: Wasim Akram
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation

                                  Highest wicket-taker in ODI for Pakistan.

                                  Wasim Akram (502 wickets).

                                  Highest wicket-taker in T20 for Pakistan.

                                  Shadab Khan (104 wickets).

                                  Highest wicket-taker in TEST for Pakistan.

                                  Wasim Akram (414 wickets)



                                  اس سوال کو ضرور پڑھیں

                                  Who scored highest Innings score in ODI for Pakistan?

                                  پاکستان میں سب سے زیادہ سکور کس نے کیا


                                  168

                                  In which city Pakistan Ordinance Factory is located?

                                  Correct Answer: Wah
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation

                                  The Pakistan Ordnance Factories (POF) is a major firearm and defense contractor

                                  headquartered in Wah Cantt, Punjab, Pakistan

                                  The POF was founded by the Government of Pakistan

                                  Founded 1951

                                  Owner: Pakistan Ministry of Defence



                                  169

                                  How many nautical miles does Pakistan include in its Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ)?

                                  Correct Answer: 200 nmi
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation

                                  Pakistan’s EEZ extends 200 nautical miles from its coastline.

                                  Within this zone, Pakistan has exclusive rights over marine resources and economic activities.

                                  170

                                  The Single National Curriculum (SNC) in Pakistan was implemented at the primary level in ______?

                                  Correct Answer: 2021
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  SNC was first applied to primary classes (1–5) in 2021 across Pakistan.
                                  The second phase started in March 2022 for classes 6–8, and later for classes 9–12.
                                  171

                                  Pakistan lies between the longitudes _______?

                                  Correct Answer: 61 E to 75.5 E
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  Pakistan lies between latitudes 24°N to 37°N.
                                  Pakistan lies between longitudes 61°E to 75.5°E.
                                  Pakistan is divided into five physiographical divisions.
                                  Islamabad is the capital of Pakistan.
                                  The construction of Islamabad was completed in 1966.
                                  The total number of longitudes is 360.
                                  There are 179 eastern longitudes and 179 western longitudes.
                                  The Prime Meridian (0° longitude) is known as the GMT (Greenwich Mean Time) line.
                                  GMT line divides the Eastern and Western Hemispheres.
                                  172

                                  Who was the first and the last Chief Executive in the history of Pakistan?

                                  Correct Answer: General Parvaiz Musharraf
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  The first and last person to hold this title was General Pervez Musharraf. 
                                  First Chief Executive: General Pervez Musharraf took the title of Chief Executive after overthrowing Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif in a bloodless coup on October 12, 1999.
                                  Last Chief Executive: He held this position until he formally appointed himself President of Pakistan in June 2001.
                                  He later voluntarily transferred the powers of chief executive to Prime Minister Zafarullah Khan Jamali in 2002. This made him both the first and last person to hold the specific designation of Chief Executive. 
                                  173

                                  Name the Pakistani police inspector who won gold medal in Powerlifting Championship 2025 by defeating her Indian rival?

                                  Correct Answer: Irum Khanum
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation

                                  Pakistani (Punjab) police female Inspector Irum Khanum won two gold medals at world powerlifting Championship 2025.

                                  She secured first position in both the powerlifting and deadlift events, defeating Indian competitor Sapna to claim the titles.

                                  She belongs to Rawalpindi Police. 


                                  Additional Information:

                                  The event was held in Colombo, Sri Lanka. The competition ran from November 27 to 30, 2025.

                                  ***

                                  174

                                  Who served as the President of Pakistan from 1971 to 1973?

                                  Correct Answer: Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation

                                  Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto was a Pakistani barrister and politician who served as the ninth prime minister of Pakistan from 1973 to 1977, and prior to that as the fourth president of Pakistan from 1971 to 1973.

                                  ****

                                  175

                                  Who is the Current Governor of Balochistan?

                                  Correct Answer: Jaffar Khan Mandokhail
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation

                                  Jaffar Khan Mandokhail is the current and 24 Governer of Balochistan

                                  Party name: PML-N

                                  Governor Balochistan since: 04 May 2024

                                  Predecessor: Abdul Wali Kakar

                                  Last Verified and Updated on 11-01-2026

                                  ان سوالات کو ضرور پڑھیں

                                  ·        Current Governor KPK

                                  ·         Current Governor of Sindh

                                  ·        Current Governor of Punjab

                                  ·        Current Governor of Balochistan

                                  176

                                  Who was the First Speaker of National Assembly of Pakistan?

                                  Correct Answer: Quaid-i-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation

                                  The first Speaker of the National Assembly was Quid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah.

                                  Note

                                  Quaid-i-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah and Moulvi Tamizuddin Khan served as the President of the Constituent Assembly, a role similar to that of the Speaker, as the official title of 'Speaker' had not yet been established at that time.

                                  ***

                                  List of speakers of the National Assembly of Pakistan

                                  1. Quid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah (11-08-1947 to 11-09-1948)
                                  2. Maulvi Tamizuddin Khan (1948–1954)
                                  3. Abdul Wahab Khan (1955–1958)
                                  4. Muhammad Ibrahim Khan (1962–1965)
                                  5. Abdul Jabbar Khan (1965–1969)
                                  6. Sahibzada Farooq Ali (1973–1977)
                                  7. Malik Meraj Khalid (1977–1977)
                                  8. Fazal Elahi Chaudhry (1972–1973)
                                  9. Syed Fakhar Imam (1985–1986)
                                  10. Hamid Nasir Chattha (1986–1988)
                                  11. Malik Anwar Ali Noon (1988–1988)
                                  12. Gohar Ayub Khan (1990–1993)
                                  13. Yusuf Raza Gillani (1993–1997)
                                  14. Elahi Bux Soomro (1997–2001)
                                  15. Chaudhry Amir Hussain (2002–2008)
                                  16. Fahmida Mirza (2008–2013)
                                  17. Sardar Ayaz Sadiq (2013–2015, 2015–2018)
                                  18. Asad Qaiser (2018–2022)
                                  19. Raja Pervaiz Ashraf (2022–2024)
                                  20. Sardar Ayaz Sadiq (2024 to till date)

                                  Source Click here

                                  177

                                  What is the length of border between Pakistan and Iran?

                                  Correct Answer: 909 km
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation

                                  NEW PAKISTAN BOUNDARIES DETAILED ACCORDING TO ASURVEY OF PAKISTAN


                                  ******

                                  ND4-12-2022

                                  ***

                                  ND16-1-2023

                                  178

                                  Pakistan to celebrate ‘Surprise Day’ on _______?

                                  Correct Answer: 27 February
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation

                                  Pakistan Air Force celebrates Operation Swift Retort as Surprise on 27 February since 2020.

                                  After 14 February 2019, when suicide bomber attacked CRPF convoy in Pulwama, killing 40 CRPF soldiers, tensions between India and Pakistan escalated.

                                  Later on February 26, 2019 Indian Air Force (IAF) carried out counter-terror operation (Balakot Air Strike), hitting in Balakot.

                                  It was on February 27, 2019, when PAF exhibited supreme professionalism by shooting down two Indian Air Force jets, a MiG-21 Bison and a SU-30. The downed MiG-21 pilot, wing commander Abhinandan, whose aircraft had crashed inside Pakistan’s territory was captured.

                                  Abhinandan was later released and handed over to India on March 1, 2019.

                                  179

                                  When sixth Census was held in Pakistan?

                                  Correct Answer: 2017
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation

                                  The sixth Census was held in Pakistan in 2017.

                                  Total Population of Pakistan in 2017 was 207.7 Million

                                  Total Males  106.34 Million

                                  Total Females 101.34 Million

                                  Growth rates 2.40%

                                  Literacy rate 59.13%.

                                  **************

                                  First Census was held in Pakistan in 1951.

                                  The second Census was held in Pakistan in 1961.

                                  The third Census was held in Pakistan in 1972.

                                  Fourth Census was held in Pakistan in 1981.

                                  The fifth Census was held in Pakistan in 1998.

                                  Sixth Census was held in Pakistan in 2017.

                                  Seventh in 2023


                                  180

                                  Rann of Kutch is a dispute area between Pakistani provence?

                                  Correct Answer: Sindh-India
                                  Show explanation
                                  Explanation
                                  • The Indo-Pakistani War of 1965 involved skirmishes between April and September 1965.
                                    • Pakistan claims the 24th Parallel as the boundary in the Rann of Kutch, but India does not recognize it.
                                    • The "Treaty of Rann of Kutch" was signed on 30 June 1965, resolving the territorial dispute.
                                    181

                                    Attock fort was built by _____?

                                    Correct Answer: Akbar
                                    Show explanation
                                    Explanation
                                    • Attock Fort, built during the reign of Akbar from 1581 to 1583 at Attock Khurd.
                                      • It was constructed to protect the River Indus's passage.
                                      • It was built under the supervision of Khawaja Shamsuddin Khawafi.
                                      • The fort features four gates: Delhi Gate, Lahori Gate, Kabuli Gate, and Mori Gate.
                                      182

                                      Annulment of partition of Bangal was made in _____?

                                      Correct Answer: 1911
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation

                                      Ø  On the visiting His Majesty King George V to Indo-Pakistan and holding of Darbar at Delhi on 12th December 1911 the partition of Bengal was cancelled.

                                      Ø  ****

                                      Ø  Partition of Bangal Announced on 19 July 1905 by Lord Curzon for Administrative requirements

                                      Ø  Congress protest against Partition of Bangal
                                      183

                                      Under the Pakistan–KSA Prisoner Transfer Agreement, how many Pakistani prisoners will be transferred back to Pakistan?

                                      Correct Answer: 2107
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation

                                      The agreement allows Pakistani prisoners to serve their remaining sentences in Pakistan.

                                      A total of 2107 prisoners are covered under this transfer arrangement.

                                      184

                                      CPEC stands for _____?

                                      Correct Answer: China Pakistan economic corridor
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation

                                      CPEC stands for China-Pakistan economic corridor

                                      **

                                      China–Pakistan Economic Corridor is a 3000 km framework of regional connection between China and Pakistan

                                      Type of project------->Economic corridor

                                      Location-------> Pakistan: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Gilgit-Baltistan, Punjab, Balochistan, Sindh & Azad Kashmir, China: Xinjiang

                                      Established---->20 April 2015 

                                      ***


                                      185

                                      Which movement of East Pakistan also supported India in war of 1971?

                                      Correct Answer: Mukti Bahini
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation

                                      The Mukti Bahini was the guerrilla resistance movement consisting of

                                      • the Bangladeshi military,
                                      • paramilitary and
                                      • civilians

                                      during the War of Liberation, which transformed East Pakistan into Bangladesh in 1971.


                                      *******

                                      ð  The war of 1971 between India and Pakistan lasted for 13 days from 3 December 1971 to the fall of Dacca (Dhaka) on 16 December 1971 .

                                      ð  The war began with Operation Chengiz Khan's preemptive aerial strikes (india).

                                      ð  on 16 December 1971 in Dhaka, marking the formation of East Pakistan as the new nation of Bangladesh.

                                      ð  Officially, East Pakistan had earlier called for its secession from Pakistan on 26 March 1971.


                                      186

                                      In which agreement the ceasefire line in Kashmir was named Line of Control?

                                      Correct Answer: Simla Agreement
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation

                                      According to the United Nations, the LOC came into effect in July 1972, after India and Pakistan fought a war that began in 1971.


                                      They signed the Simla Agreement to define what was previously called the “ceasefire line.”

                                      ***************

                                      vu FIA CBT PAPER 15-12-2021

                                      187

                                      Who was the head of Awami League in 1970?

                                      Correct Answer: Mujib ur Rehman
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation

                                      Sheikh Mujib-Ur-Rehman served as the first President and later as the Prime Minister of Bangladesh from 17 April 1971 until his assassination on 15 August 1975.

                                      188

                                      Kharan desert is located in which province?

                                      Correct Answer: Balochistan
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation

                                      The Kharan Desert is a sandy and mountainous desert situated in Balochistan Province in south-western Pakistan.

                                      The Kharan desert in Balochistan near the border of Iran.

                                      *************

                                      189

                                      Jahangir Khan is a famous player in which game?

                                      Correct Answer: Squash
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation

                                      Jahangir Khan is a former World No. 1 professional squash player from Pakistan.

                                      He won the World Open title six times.

                                      He won the British Open title ten times, from 1982 to 1991.

                                      He was born into a Pashtun family from Neway Kelay Payan, Peshawar.

                                      He served as President of the World Squash Federation from 2002 to 2008.

                                      Jahangir Khan retired as a player in 1993.

                                      190

                                      Who is the writer of Urdu Book “Pakistan Naguzeer tha”?

                                      Correct Answer: Syed Hasan Riaz
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation

                                      Syed Hassan Raza is a Pakistani diplomat and a former senior career foreign service officer.

                                      He served as the Special Secretary at Pakistan's Foreign Office, overseeing the Middle East, OIC & Legal divisions

                                      191

                                      What was the objective of Cripps Mission?

                                      Correct Answer: To reform the constitutional issues
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation

                                      The Cripps Mission was a failed attempt in late March 1942 

                                      The Cripps Mission was sent by the British government to India

                                        to obtain Indian cooperation for the British war efforts in the 2nd World War.

                                      The mission was headed by a senior minister Sir Stafford Cripps.

                                      the objective of Cripps Mission: To reform the constitutional issues


                                      **
                                      ND13-1-2023


                                      192

                                      When was FATA merged into Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (KPK)?

                                      Correct Answer: 2018
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation

                                      FATA was merged with KPK in May 2018.

                                      FATA stands for Federally Administered Tribal Areas

                                      FATA was merged with KPK under 25th amendment. 

                                      After the merger of the Federally Administered Tribal Areas with KPK, FATA seats were abolished and there will be no election on 4 of the 8 seats. The rest of the 4 seats will also not be renewed in 2024.

                                      193

                                      Which of the following first President of the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan?

                                      Correct Answer: Quaid-e-Azam
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation

                                      On 11th August 1947 Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah was elected as the President of the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan.


                                      The first session of the Constituent Assembly of Pakistan was held on 10th August 1947 at the Sindh Assembly Building in Karachi.


                                      Liaquat Ali Khan headed after the death of Jinnah for three years and produced the Objectives Resolution.

                                      ****


                                      194

                                      Who is the Head of the State of Pakistan?

                                      Correct Answer: President
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation

                                      The President of Pakistan is the head of state of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan.


                                      The current head of state of Pakistan is Asif Ali Zardari who was elected in 2024.


                                      195

                                      سب سے پہلے نعرہ “پاکستان کا مطلب کیا؟ لا الٰہ الا اللہ” کس نے لگایا؟

                                      Correct Answer: اصغر صہبائی (اصغر سودائی)
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation

                                      یہ نعرہ سب سے پہلے اصغر صہبائی/اصغر سودائی نے دیا۔

                                      بعد میں یہ نعرہ تحریکِ پاکستان کی پہچان بن گیا۔

                                      196

                                      Who completed the construction of Qutb Minar at Delhi?

                                      Correct Answer: Iltutmish
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation

                                      Qutub minar constructed by: Qutb Uddin Aibak

                                      Construction of Qutub minar started in: 1199

                                      Construction of Qutb Minar completed by: Iltutmish

                                      197

                                      One Unit Bill was passed in which year?

                                      Correct Answer: 1955
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation

                                      The One Unit was the reorganization of the provinces of Pakistan .

                                      Prime Minister Muhammad Ali Bogra led it on 22 November 1954.

                                      and passed on 30 September 1955

                                      This program merged the four provinces of West Pakistan into a single province to parallel the province of East Pakistan

                                      • West Punjab
                                      • Sind
                                      • NWFP
                                      • Baluchistan

                                      Additional information:

                                      The One Unit scheme was dissolved on July 1, 1970

                                      General Yahya Khan imposed Legal Framework Order No. 1970 to end the program

                                      and reinstate the provisional status of the Four Provinces as of August 1947.

                                      198

                                      Which Mountain range is often called as the Third Pole?

                                      Correct Answer: Hindu Kush Himalayan range
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation

                                      The mountain range often called the "Third Pole" is the Hindu Kush Himalaya (HKH) region,

                                      They contain the largest area of permanent ice cover outside of the Arctic and Antarctic. 

                                       Additional information 

                                      The Hindu Kush range starts near the Pamir Mountains,

                                      The Tibetan Plateau (Pamir Plateau) is known as the Roof of the World.

                                      Where the borders of Afghanistan, Pakistan, and China meet.

                                      From this eastern point, it runs southwest through Pakistan and into Afghanistan. 

                                      It is an 800-kilometre-long (500 mi) mountain range in Central and South Asia to the west of the Himalayas.

                                      The highest peak of the Hindu Kush range in Pakistan is Tirich Mir

                                      Elevation:  (7,708) meters (25,289) ft

                                      199

                                      When was the Constitution of 1973 enforced?

                                      Correct Answer: 14th August 1973
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation

                                      Constitution of 1973

                                      Zulifqar Ali Bhutto introduced new constitution in Pakistan on August 14, 1973.

                                      According to 1973 constitution, both national assembly and senate elect president for Pakistan whereas the prime minister is selected by national assembly.

                                      Bicameral Legislature system was introduced for the first time in Pakistan in the constitution of 1973.

                                      ******

                                      1956 Constitution

                                      March 23, 1956 by PM Chaudhry Mohammad Ali + GG Iskander Mirza

                                      1962 Constitution

                                      8th June 1962 by President Ayub Khan.

                                      1973 Constitution

                                      14th Aug 1973, by Zulfikar Ali Bhutto

                                      200

                                      Who was the first Chairman of Senate?

                                      Correct Answer: Habib Ullah Khan
                                      Show explanation
                                      Explanation

                                      About Senate of Pakistan


                                      Senate: Upper House of Pakistan

                                      Senate in Urdu: ایوان بالا

                                      Formation: 1973

                                      Original members: 45

                                      Raised to 63: 1977

                                      Raised 87: 2 March, 1985

                                      Raised to 100: 2002

                                      First Hindu Woman Senator: Kirshna Kumari

                                      Half of the Senate members retire after: 3 years

                                      Members term: 6 years

                                      Head of Senate is called: Chairman

                                      National Assembly & Senate: Makes up the Parliament of Pakistan

                                      Seats for each province: 23

                                      Baluchistan Women Seats: 4

                                      Sindh Women Seats: 4

                                      KPK Women Seats: 4

                                      Punjab Women Seats: 4

                                      ICT Women Seat: 1

                                      Total Women Seats: 17

                                      Senate elections: after 3 years

                                      First woman deputy chairperson: Dr Noor Jahan Panezai

                                      Youngest chairman senate: Sadiq Sanjrani

                                      First chairman Senate: Habib Ullah Khan




                                      Nice work getting this far. Consistency beats intensity — come back tomorrow for the next lesson.
